metric.name: parse_throughput
metric.definition: parse-only throughput over 2000 repo .py files; no encode, no per-file subprocess. This is the slice the '109x' claim came from.

verdict: Even the FASTEST parser (ruff) is ~6.3x Python ast.parse here, not 109x. The 109x figure does not reproduce in any configuration measured: full encode end-to-end ~4.5x (local + Kaggle agree), startup-excluded engine ~4.66x, parse-only ruff-vs-Python ~6.3x, rustpython-vs-Python ~2.0x.
notes: Laptop, unpinned; MB differs slightly between runners due to byte-count encoding (surrogatepass). Throughput ratio is the comparable figure.
